checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K income statement and cash flow statement
10-K income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueNet=462320000 (2012 annual) vs 470121000 (2011 annual), a 1.66% decline

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: 10-Q/10-K receivables footnote, showing reserve growth not decline

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=185175000 (2009-12-31, filed 2012-02-29) vs Goodwill=73598000 (2010-12-31, filed 2013-03-01) - materially different figures for adjacent fiscal year-ends reported in different filings
evidence.location: 10-K FY2011 selected financial data vs 0001193125-13-087561 10-K FY2012 balance sheet
10-Q Q3 2011 balance sheet vs 0001193125-13-087561 10-K FY2012 comparative balance sheet

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans annual and quarterly points from 2008-12-31 through 2013-03-31 across Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, NetIncomeLoss, CashFlow, Inventory, Receivables, Goodwill, Intangibles
NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities and NetCashProvidedByUsedInInvestingActivities data stop after 2011 annual figures, limiting full cash-flow reconciliation for 2012 asset/liability swings
evidence.location: 10-K FY2011 cash flow statement (no 2012 equivalent provided)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature/aggressive revenue recognition or distributor channel stuffing in FY2012: receivables grew 13.2% while revenue fell ~1.7% and net income (51,295,000) far exceeded operating cash flow (11,206,000), consistent with revenue being booked ahead of cash collection or with collectability issues not yet reserved.

Prior-period overstatement of goodwill/intangibles later corrected: figures reported in FY2011 10-K and interim 10-Qs (Goodwill ~$180-185M, Intangibles ~$39-41M) are dramatically higher than the same fiscal-year-end balances later reported in the FY2012 10-K (Goodwill ~$73-74M, Intangibles ~$7-8M), consistent with an unrecognized impairment or reclassification error being corrected retroactively (a restatement-like pattern), which would have overstated historical total assets and equity.

rationale_evidence.location: 10-Q Q3 2011 vs 10-K FY2012 comparative balance sheet

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Insufficient/delayed inventory obsolescence reserves: inventory grew 6.9% in FY2012 while revenue declined 1.66%, suggesting slow-moving or excess inventory not being written down promptly, which would understate cost of goods sold and overstate inventory asset value.

top_signals: Receivables grew 13.2% in FY2012 (AR=150,316,000 vs 132,828,000) while revenue declined 1.66% (462,320,000 vs 470,121,000), a classic revenue-quality red flag
Net income (51,295,000, FY2012) diverged sharply from operating cash flow (11,206,000, FY2012), a ~$40M gap suggesting earnings not backed by cash generation

Materially different goodwill and intangible asset balances reported for the same/adjacent fiscal year-ends across different filings (Goodwill ~$180-185M in original 2011 filings vs ~$73-74M in the FY2012 10-K; Intangibles ~$39M vs ~$8M), indicating a large retroactive correction/restatement of prior-period asset values

Multiple 10-K/A amendments filed over the company's history (2009, 2011, 2012), along with recurring SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD comment-letter cycles, indicating a pattern of accounting corrections and regulatory scrutiny
Inventory build-up (+6.9% FY2012) against declining revenue, suggesting potential inventory valuation/obsolescence issues
